summary: Cloudflare deployment acceptance now consumes exact proof bytes, an evidence envelope, the in-toto statement, deny-by-default policy, expected source identity, and normalized external signature verification instead of reducing verifier outputs to caller-supplied booleans.
finding: The prior Cloudflare adapter converted verifier statuses into booleans before invoking the Frontier R-005 gate. After the gate API changed to artifact-bound inputs, that boundary was both semantically unsafe and interface-incompatible.
design_implication: Deployment acceptance is now fail-closed across artifact binding, provenance identity, signature verification, and served-deployment claim evidence. Diagnostic status fields remain available for workflow summaries but are not used as the provenance acceptance oracle.

verification.method: repository source inspection and exact commit status query
verification.result: source integration committed; no CI statuses were present for the final commit at inspection time

limits: No GitHub Actions execution is claimed for the new commits.
No Cloudflare deployment occurred because C-001 remains blocked on environment credentials and dispatch.
Artifact/provenance acceptance does not prove scientific truth.
A valid signed artifact is rejected as deployment evidence when the served identity claim is invalid.
falsification_routes: Run cloudflare-static/build-evidence-acceptance.test.mjs in GitHub Actions and inspect all assertions.
Alter one byte of cloudflare-deployment-proof.json and confirm subject.digest and envelope.artifact-binding rejection.
Supply a valid signed proof with no successful served-identity row and confirm claim.invalid rejection.
Dispatch the Cloudflare workflow after credential configuration and inspect the uploaded acceptance artifact.
